description:
# Comprehensive Testing and Hardware Validation

## Purpose
Verify the refactoring works correctly across all environments.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests (Local)
```bash
# Each driver crate independently
cargo test -p daq-driver-mock
cargo test -p daq-driver-thorlabs
cargo test -p daq-driver-newport
cargo test -p daq-driver-spectra-physics

# Framework
cargo test -p daq-hardware
cargo test -p daq-drivers
```

### 2. Integration Tests (Local with Mocks)
```bash
# Full system with mock drivers
cargo test -p daq-bin --features mock

# Registry dynamic dispatch
cargo test --test registry_integration
```

### 3. Hardware Tests (maitai)
```bash
ssh maitai@100.117.5.12
cd ~/rust-daq
source scripts/env-check.sh

# Individual driver hardware tests
cargo test -p daq-driver-thorlabs --features hardware_tests
cargo test -p daq-driver-newport --features hardware_tests
cargo test -p daq-driver-spectra-physics --features hardware_tests
cargo test -p daq-driver-pvcam --features pvcam_hardware

# Full daemon startup
cargo build --release -p daq-bin --features maitai
./target/release/rust-daq-daemon daemon \
  --port 50051 \
  --hardware-config config/maitai_hardware.toml
```

### 4. Backward Compatibility Tests
```bash
# Verify existing configs work unchanged
cargo run -p daq-bin --features maitai -- \
  --hardware-config config/maitai_hardware.toml \
  --dry-run

# Expected: No config changes needed
```

### 5. Feature Combination Tests
```bash
# Minimal build
cargo check -p daq-bin --no-default-features

# All features
cargo check -p daq-bin --features all

# Specific combinations
cargo check -p daq-bin --features "thorlabs,mock"
```

## Test Matrix

| Test | Local | CI | maitai |
|------|-------|-----|--------|
| daq-driver-mock unit | ✓ | ✓ | - |
| daq-driver-thorlabs unit | ✓ | ✓ | - |
| daq-driver-thorlabs hw | - | - | ✓ |
| daq-driver-newport unit | ✓ | ✓ | - |
| daq-driver-newport hw | - | - | ✓ |
| daq-driver-spectra-physics unit | ✓ | ✓ | - |
| daq-driver-spectra-physics hw | - | - | ✓ |
| daq-driver-pvcam unit | ✓ | ✓ | - |
| daq-driver-pvcam hw | - | - | ✓ |
| registry integration | ✓ | ✓ | - |
| daemon startup | - | - | ✓ |
| GUI connection | - | - | ✓ |

## Regression Checklist
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] All integration tests pass
- [ ] ELL14 rotators work (multiple on same bus)
- [ ] ESP300 axes work
- [ ] Newport 1830-C reads power
- [ ] MaiTai responds to commands
- [ ] PVCAM streams frames
- [ ] GUI connects and displays frames
- [ ] gRPC API unchanged
- [ ] Existing TOML configs work

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% test pass rate
- [ ] No regressions from before refactor
- [ ] Hardware validated on maitai
- [ ] Release build works correctly

close_reason: Implemented defense-in-depth shutter safety: ShutterRegistry for emergency shutdown, HeartbeatShutterGuard for timeout protection, signal handlers for SIGTERM/SIGINT. Documented SIGKILL/power-failure limitations. Hardware interlocks still recommended for production.
notes:
## Implementation Complete

### Files Changed

1. **NEW: `crates/daq-scripting/src/shutter_safety.rs`**
   - `ShutterRegistry` - Global registry of open shutters for emergency shutdown
   - `HeartbeatShutterGuard` - Timeout-based shutter guard (closes if no heartbeat)
   - Signal handlers (SIGTERM/SIGINT) that close all shutters on graceful shutdown
   - Panic hook installation for emergency shutdown

2. **MODIFIED: `crates/daq-scripting/src/bindings.rs`**
   - Updated `with_shutter_open()` to register with ShutterRegistry
   - Added comprehensive safety documentation (bd-ykrq)
   - Shutters now unregistered on closure

3. **MODIFIED: `crates/daq-scripting/src/lib.rs`**
   - Exported new `shutter_safety` module
   - Added public exports: `HeartbeatShutterGuard`, `ShutterRegistry`, `DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT`

4. **MODIFIED: `crates/daq-scripting/Cargo.toml`**
   - Added `signal` feature to tokio for signal handling

### New Safety Layers

```
Defense in Depth:
Layer 1: with_shutter_open()     - Rust control flow (existing)
Layer 2: ShutterRegistry         - Emergency close-all for signals/panics (NEW)
Layer 3: HeartbeatShutterGuard   - Timeout-based closure for hangs (NEW)
Layer 4: Hardware interlock      - EXTERNAL (recommended, not software)
```

### Test Results

All 52 tests in daq-scripting pass, including:
- `test_heartbeat_guard_opens_shutter` - Verifies guard opens shutter
- `test_heartbeat_prevents_timeout` - Heartbeats keep shutter open
- `test_timeout_closes_shutter` - No heartbeat triggers auto-close
- `test_registry_register_unregister` - Registry operations work

### Remaining Limitations

The following cases CANNOT be protected by software:
- SIGKILL (kill -9)
- OOM killer
- Power failure
- Hardware crash

**Recommendation**: For production laser labs, always use hardware interlocks.

### Usage Examples

```rust
// Install signal handlers at startup
ShutterRegistry::install_signal_handlers();
ShutterRegistry::install_panic_hook();

// Use heartbeat guard for long operations
let guard = HeartbeatShutterGuard::new(driver, Duration::from_secs(5)).await?;
loop {
    guard.heartbeat();  // Must call periodically!
    do_work();
}
// Auto-closes on drop OR timeout
```
title: with_shutter_open() doesn't protect against process hangs

close_reason: TDD tests created by Gemini, cross-validated by Codex. Benchmark measures P50/P90/P99 latency under baseline, blocking, and async loads. Tests demonstrate blocking code degradation.
description: Create a benchmark that simulates heavy hardware I/O and measures gRPC responsiveness. Should fail/show spikes under current architecture.
design:
IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
1. File: crates/daq-bin/tests/latency_benchmark.rs
2. Harness: Start rust-daq-daemon with Mock Hardware.
3. Test Case A (Baseline): Concurrent gRPC 'Move' and 'Read' requests without artificial load. Measure P50, P90, P99 latency.
4. Test Case B (Degraded): Repeat Case A while a background 'StarvationTask' performs std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)) in a loop.
5. Test Case C (Target): Repeat Case A while background task uses spawn_blocking for the sleep operation.
6. Metric: Quantify the 'Jitter' introduced by Case B compared to Case C.
notes: NEXT: Implement the integration test harness using 'tonic' client and 'tokio::spawn'.
title: TDD: Create Latency & Load Benchmark

description:
CRITICAL: Unverified unsafe impl Send + Sync for AxisHandle.

Location: crates/driver-dover-motion/src/driver.rs lines 36-40

Current code claims thread-safety without documentation proof.

Research findings from docs/reference/markdown/dover-motion-api-manual.md:
1. SDK has internal dispatcher thread (line 7858)
2. Callbacks run on different thread (line 6830)
3. No explicit thread-safety guarantees found

Fix:
1. KEEP Send - handles can move between threads
2. REMOVE Sync - no evidence of concurrent access safety  
3. The Arc<Mutex<AxisHandle>> wrapper already serializes access
4. Update SAFETY comment to document conservative interpretation

Also fix Drop impl (line 275-283) to call SDK shutdown properly.

```rust
// SAFETY: device_index is validated in-range by num_devices check above.
// The buffer is stack-allocated with fixed size 256 which matches SDK expectations.
// No concurrent access - we hold &mut self.
unsafe { ShamrockGetSerialNumber(...) }
```
